issues: `python -m mypy src` fails (13 strict-typing errors) in `src/moe_surgeon/schemas.py` (e.g., TypeVar binding mismatch at `src/moe_surgeon/schemas.py:58`, invalid return types/uses at `:78`, `:85`, `:239`, `:333-335`, tuple/list coercion issues in `_coerce_mapping_for_payload`, and `sort_topology` return annotation mismatch at `src/moe_surgeon/schemas.py:956`). This violates the project quality gate and P1 strict typing expectation.

`python -m moe_surgeon` is not executable and exits `No module named moe_surgeon.__main__`, so the requested `python -m moe_surgeon`-style import/run path is currently broken (`src/moe_surgeon/__init__.py` exists but no `__main__.py`).

Deterministic canonical JSON is not stable for default-constructed objects across processes: default timestamp fields (`created_at`/`started_at`) are auto-generated at init (`src/moe_surgeon/schemas.py:354`, `:828`), so two equivalent `PrunePlan()` instances in different runs produce different JSON payloads (`python -m`, verified with back-to-back process checks). This undermines reproducibility requirements for canonical outputs.

The provided execution/review claims are inconsistent with collected evidence: prior summary states quality checks/tests are fully passing, but rerun shows `python -m mypy src` fails and JSON reproducibility of default objects is not stable. Treating those claims as ground truth without rerun would be incorrect.
